codeReview.summary: The settlement refactor moves the milestone to explicit header-level behavior rather than legacy line inference: requests now carry amount-driven allocation rows with DOCUMENT, ON_ACCOUNT, and FUTURE_APPLICATION typing, controller helpers enforce header/body idempotency-key parity, and the settlement engine validates duplicate-target conflicts while reconstructing replay-safe results from stored allocations. The reviewed tests cover concurrent dealer replay, supplier/dealer allocation validation, and controller idempotency handling, and I did not find a blocking mismatch with the header-level settlement contract.

summary: Pass. Dealer and supplier settlements now use replay-safe header allocations with explicit on-account or future-application rows, and the reviewed controller/runtime coverage supports FIFO or oldest-open defaults without duplicate settlement truth.
